name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc.

profile.description: Advanced Energy Industries designs and manufactures precision power conversion, measurement, and control solutions for critical applications in semiconductor fabrication, data center computing, telecom infrastructure, and industrial markets. The company generated $1.8B in revenue in 2025.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: AEIS delivered 107% YoY growth in Data Center Computing revenue to $587M, driven by customized high-power-density power conversion solutions adopted by hyperscalers for AI rack applications. The company's new products enable advanced semiconductor node processes at 2nm and below. A newly built Thailand plant provides $1B+ incremental revenue capacity. This is a world-class innovation engine serving the most demanding technology applications.

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: AEIS's mission to enable breakthrough technologies through precision power is embodied in its products powering the world's most advanced AI data centers and semiconductor fabs. CEO Steve Kelley's leadership has driven a transformation from a semiconductor-dependent company to a diversified power platform. The 20% revenue growth and 70% EPS growth in 2025 energize stakeholder confidence.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Revenue grew 21% to $1.8B with gross margins reaching nearly 40% (best in 5 years). Full-year adjusted EBITDA rose 68% to $324M. Record operating cash flow of $235M demonstrates strong cash generation. Daily dollar volume exceeds $221M, providing exceptional liquidity. However, P/E of 82x is stretched, debt-to-equity of 49.8% is notable, and the stock has run up 9.4% above its 50-day SMA, suggesting near-term overvaluation risk.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI is the primary growth driver for AEIS. Data Center Computing revenue more than doubled in 2025, and the company's customized power solutions are being adopted by hyperscalers for AI rack applications. AEIS is essential infrastructure for AI data centers — AI systems require precisely controlled power delivery, and AEIS's high-power-density conversion technology is purpose-built for this market. Management guides 30%+ data center revenue growth in 2026.

infinite_game.summary: AEIS demonstrates strong infinite game characteristics through strategic diversification from semiconductor dependence to a multi-market power platform, long-term capacity investments (Thailand factory), and technology development enabling the next generation of semiconductor and AI infrastructure.

just_cause.analysis: Enabling breakthrough technologies through precision power conversion is a compelling just cause that connects the company to society's most important technological advances. The company's products directly enable AI computing, advanced chip manufacturing, and clean energy systems.

trusting_teams.analysis: CEO Steve Kelley has built a high-performing team that delivered 20% revenue growth, 70% EPS growth, and margin expansion simultaneously. The 12,000-employee organization has executed the strategic pivot to data centers while maintaining semiconductor leadership. The Thailand factory investment demonstrates commitment to workforce growth.

worthy_rivals.analysis: AEIS competes with power companies like Delta Electronics, Vicor, and Infineon in data center power, and with established semiconductor equipment suppliers. The company explicitly targets market share gains through new products at 2nm and below, acknowledging competitive dynamics transparently.

existential_flexibility.analysis: The strategic pivot from semiconductor dependence to diversified power platform — with Data Center Computing growing from minimal revenue to $587M (107% YoY) — demonstrates extraordinary existential flexibility. The Thailand factory investment creates optionality for $1B+ additional capacity.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Setting a long-term gross margin target of 43%, investing in a new Thailand factory during an uncertain macro environment, and guiding 30%+ data center growth for 2026 all demonstrate leadership courage. The company is making bold bets on AI infrastructure that differentiate it from peers.

investment_thesis: AEIS is a premier picks-and-shovels play on AI infrastructure, with Data Center Computing revenue doubling to $587M in 2025 and guided for 30%+ growth in 2026. The company's precision power solutions are essential for hyperscaler AI racks, and new semiconductor products for sub-2nm nodes position it for market share gains. With revenue growing 21% to $1.8B, margins at 5-year highs, record cash flow, and $2.5B+ revenue-capable infrastructure, AEIS has the platform to deliver sustained high-teens growth.
thesis_continued: The trailing P/E of 82x prices in significant future growth, creating vulnerability if AI infrastructure spending decelerates. Forward P/E of 29.8x is more reasonable but still premium. Tariff headwinds from the new Thailand factory and global supply chain could pressure margins. Semiconductor revenue grew only 6% YoY, and a longer-than-expected downturn in chip capex could offset data center gains. The company's rapid growth in data center is partially from a low base, and sustaining 30%+ growth will be increasingly challenging as the business scales.
key_catalysts: Data Center Computing revenue guided for 30%+ growth in 2026 with second wave of cloud customers engaging
New semiconductor products enabling sub-2nm processes expected to drive market share gains in 2026+
Thailand factory commissioning adding $1B+ revenue capacity to support growth trajectory
key_risks: Trailing P/E of 82x leaves significant downside if AI data center spending growth decelerates
Tariff headwinds from global manufacturing footprint could pressure margins below the 40% target
Semiconductor segment grew only 6%, and a broader chip capex downturn could offset data center gains
